source_url_status: LIVE — https://www.osha.gov/nationally-recognized-testing-laboratory-program resolves to the official OSHA NRTL program page.

corrections.issue: CFR citations incomplete / slightly mislabelled. The foundational NRTL authority is 29 CFR 1910.7 (Definition and requirements for a nationally recognised testing laboratory). 29 CFR 1910.399 contains definitions for Subpart S (electrical standards) that reference 'listed' equipment; 29 CFR 1926.403(a) is the construction-industry corollary. Both are correct as supporting citations but 29 CFR 1910.7 is the primary NRTL-recognition statute and should be listed first.
corrections.action: Add '29 CFR 1910.7 (primary NRTL recognition authority)' as the first entry in standards_or_laws.

corrections.issue: UL 62368-1 3rd Edition is now superseded. The 4th edition (ANSI/UL 62368-1 4th Ed., adoption of IEC 62368-1:2023 4th Ed.) was published July 31, 2025. Existing 3rd-edition certificates remain valid under transition provisions, but new certifications are issued to the 4th edition. The fragment should note the current edition.
corrections.action: Update '3rd Edition' reference to '3rd/4th Edition (4th Ed. published July 2025; verify current required edition with your NRTL)'.

mandatory_status_verdict: CONFIRMED — NRTL listing is mandatory for NEC-covered products sold through US retail; USB chargers / power adapters fall within scope.
gap_verdict: CONFIRMED — CCC under GB 4943.1-2022 is not accepted in the US. Separate NRTL listing required.
notes: No material errors in the substance of this record. The OSHA source URL is live and official. Minor bibliographic corrections needed on edition number and CFR primary citation.

source_url_status: CAUTION — https://www.fcc.gov/consumers/guides/fccs-role-equipment-authorization is an FCC consumer guide page. It resolves but is not the primary regulatory text. A more authoritative source for this record would be the eCFR page for 47 CFR Part 15 (https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-47/chapter-I/subchapter-A/part-15) or the FCC equipment authorization hub at https://www.fcc.gov/oet/ea/rfdevice.
source_url_fix: Replace source URL with: https://www.fcc.gov/oet/ea/rfdevice (FCC Equipment Authorization — RF Device, official OET page).

corrections.issue: Fragment lists 'USB Power Delivery Specification (USB-IF)' without a revision number and flags it [UNVERIFIED]. The current specification is Universal Serial Bus Power Delivery Specification, Revision 3.2, Version 1.2 (released 2026-05-20; equivalent to IEC 62680-1-2:2024). The previous long-current version was Rev 3.2 v1.1 (2024-10). Rev 3.1 mentioned in ganus-market-01 is one generation behind.
corrections.action: Update USB PD reference to 'USB Power Delivery Specification Rev. 3.2 v1.2 (USB-IF, 2026) — verify latest at usb.org'.

corrections.issue: Current USB Type-C Cable and Connector Specification is Release 2.4, October 2024 (not Rev 2.3 as cited in ganus-market-01).
corrections.action: Use 'USB Type-C Cable and Connector Specification Release 2.4 (USB-IF, October 2024)'.

corrections.issue: Correctly labelled 'effectively mandatory via retail contracts' (not federally mandated). In the EU, USB-IF / IEC 62680 conformance is now mandatory under the Radio Equipment Directive / Ecodesign EPS Regulation, but in the US it remains voluntary at law. Verdict: US voluntary / EU mandatory distinction is accurately stated.
corrections.action: No change required for US market description.

mandatory_status_verdict: CONFIRMED — FCC Part 15 authorization mandatory for RF-enabled chargers; Class B emission limits apply to all wired chargers; USB-IF voluntary in US law but commercially mandatory via retail contracts.
gap_verdict: CONFIRMED — Chinese SRRC and GB/T fast-charging certifications not accepted in the US.
notes: The source URL should be upgraded to the FCC OET equipment authorization page. USB PD specification version numbers updated from search results.

source_url_status: CONFIRMED — https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-47/chapter-I/subchapter-A/part-15/subpart-B is the live eCFR page for FCC 47 CFR Part 15 Subpart B (Unintentional Radiators). Direct browser fetch was blocked by a Cloudflare redirect in the automated check, but the URL is confirmed as the canonical eCFR location for this regulation by multiple search results returning the exact URL from ecfr.gov.

corrections.issue: The fragment contained [UNVERIFIED] tags around the 2025 FCC China-lab restriction claim. This claim is NOW CONFIRMED with specific details.
corrections.action: Remove [UNVERIFIED] tag and update with: 'In 2025, the FCC adopted Report and Order FCC 25-27 (ET Docket No. 24-136, adopted May 22, 2025) banning prohibited entities — including Chinese-government-controlled labs — from participating in the equipment authorization program. By end of 2025, the FCC had initiated withdrawal or denial proceedings against 15+ Chinese-government-controlled labs (including CTTL/CAICT, Dkt ET 25-267, DA 25-929; and CAIC Chongqing, Dkt ET 25-268, DA 25-930). Rules define "prohibited entity" by reference to the FCC Covered List, DoD Chinese Military Company list, and Executive Branch foreign adversary designations. Verify current list of recognised labs at fcc.gov/oet/ea before commissioning tests.'

corrections.issue: KDB 784748 is the correct KDB publication number for SDoC guidance. Confirmed as a valid FCC KDB entry.
corrections.action: No change.

corrections.field: cn_common_equivalent — ANSI C63.4 vs CISPR 32 limits
corrections.issue: The [UNVERIFIED] tag on the frequency-by-frequency limit comparison between GB/T 9254.1-2021 and FCC Part 15 Class B is appropriate — this requires a specialist gap analysis and cannot be resolved by public web search alone.
corrections.action: Retain [UNVERIFIED] caveat on limit-value comparison. Statement that the limits differ at some sub-bands is substantively correct.

mandatory_status_verdict: CONFIRMED — FCC Part 15 Subpart B Class B limits mandatory for all switching power supplies (including GaN chargers) marketed or imported for US household use.
gap_verdict: CONFIRMED — Three-gap analysis (test framework, lab recognition, SDoC/US-responsible-party) is accurate and materially complete. 2025 FCC lab restriction orders confirmed.
notes: Primary correction is to replace [UNVERIFIED] on the 2025 FCC China-lab action with confirmed docket details. Switching-frequency range claimed (100 kHz–10 MHz fundamental) is technically sound for GaN-based SMPS topologies.

source_url_status: CONFIRMED — https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-47/chapter-I/subchapter-A/part-2/subpart-J is the eCFR page for FCC 47 CFR Part 2 Subpart J (Equipment Authorization). The canonical FCC lab list URL is https://www.fcc.gov/oet/ea/rfdevice (equipment authorization hub).

corrections.issue: The 2025 FCC restriction on Chinese-government-controlled labs is CONFIRMED. Key verified facts: (1) Report and Order FCC 25-27, ET Docket No. 24-136, adopted May 22, 2025, released May 27, 2025. (2) Rules prohibit any 'prohibited entity' (defined by FCC Covered List, DoD Chinese Military Company list, foreign adversary designations) from participating in TCBs, test labs, or accreditation bodies; or holding 10%+ equity/voting interest in any such entity. (3) By October 2025, proceedings initiated against 15+ Chinese-government-controlled labs. Named labs include: CTTL / China Academy of Information and Communications Technology (CAICT), Beijing (Dkt ET 25-267, DA 25-929); Chongqing Academy of Information and Communications Technology (CAIC), Chongqing (Dkt ET 25-268, DA 25-930). Seven labs targeted in September 2025; five more in October 2025 coordinated action (DA 26-187 covers a further batch). (4) Erratum issued August 6, 2025. (5) Effective rule published in Federal Register July 16, 2025 (FR docket 2025-13308).
corrections.action: Replace all [UNVERIFIED] text in this record with the confirmed facts above. Update standards_or_laws entry to: 'FCC Report and Order FCC 25-27, ET Docket No. 24-136 (adopted May 22, 2025) — Bad Labs rule prohibiting Chinese-government-controlled lab participation in equipment authorization program. Federal Register: 2025-13308 (July 16, 2025).' Add specific named labs as examples in the gap description.

corrections.issue: Source URL points to eCFR Part 2 Subpart J. A more directly relevant source for this specific record is the FCC's own announcement page.
corrections.action: Replace source URL with: https://www.fcc.gov/document/fcc-bans-bad-labs-us-equipment-authorization-process-0 (FCC official page for Report and Order FCC 25-27). Publisher: 'U.S. Federal Communications Commission — Report and Order FCC 25-27, ET Docket No. 24-136 (May 27, 2025)'.

mandatory_status_verdict: CONFIRMED — The de facto requirement to use an FCC-accepted, non-Chinese-government-controlled lab for US SDoC testing is now codified in FCC 25-27. The practical mandatory status description is accurate.
gap_verdict: CONFIRMED AND STRENGTHENED — The structural gap is now fully verified. The claim that Chinese domestic lab data carries 'regulatory risk' is an understatement: post-FCC 25-27, Chinese-government-controlled labs are affirmatively prohibited. Manufacturers must verify lab status at fcc.gov/oet/ea before commissioning tests.
notes: This record was the most materially unverified in the dataset. All key claims are now confirmed with specific docket numbers, dates, and named labs. The [UNVERIFIED] hedges should be removed and replaced with confirmed details.

source_url_status: CONFIRMED — The eCFR URL https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-10/chapter-II/subchapter-D/part-430/subpart-B/appendix-Appendix%20Z%20to%20Subpart%20B%20of%20Part%20430 is confirmed as the live canonical location for Appendix Z to Subpart B of Part 430 (DoE Level VI test method for EPS). Direct automated fetch redirected to Cloudflare firewall but the URL is valid per multiple authoritative search results.

corrections.field: target_requirement.summary — DoE Compliance Certification Database naming
corrections.issue: The summary refers to a 'DoE Compliance Certification Database (CCD)'. DoE's official system is the Compliance Certification Management System (CCMS), and the searchable public database is the Compliance Certification Database (CCD). Both exist: CCMS is the submission portal; CCD is the public-facing search database. The fragment uses 'CCD' in ganus-market-02 but does not name it consistently here.
corrections.action: Clarify: 'Products must be enrolled in DoE's Compliance Certification Management System (CCMS) — the public-facing database is the Compliance Certification Database (CCD), searchable at energy.gov — before being distributed in US commerce.'

corrections.issue: Fragment states Level VI took effect 'February 10, 2016' in ganus-market-02. The DoE final rule for Level VI efficiency standards was published in February 2014 (EERE-2008-BT-STD-0005) with compliance required as of February 10, 2016. The test procedure (Appendix Z) was amended in a final rule published August 25, 2015. Both dates are consistent with the 2016 compliance effective date cited in ganus-market-02. The efficiency-only record (doe-level-vi-eps) does not state an effective date explicitly — that is acceptable.
corrections.action: No change needed to doe-level-vi-eps on effective date. Confirm ganus-market-02 effective date of 2016-02-10 is CONFIRMED.

corrections.issue: Correctly states mandatory, federal, DoE-enforced. The EPCA authority (42 U.S.C. § 6291 et seq.) is accurate. CBP enforcement of DoE EPS standards is confirmed (DoE works with CBP for import enforcement).
corrections.action: No change.

mandatory_status_verdict: CONFIRMED — DoE Level VI is a mandatory federal requirement for EPS imported or sold in the US. Non-compliant products are subject to civil penalties. CBP may detain non-enrolled EPS shipments.
gap_verdict: CONFIRMED — GB 20943 compliance does not satisfy DoE Level VI. Test methods and efficiency formulas differ. DoE efficiency mark (Roman numeral VI) is a distinct labelling obligation with no GB 20943 equivalent.
notes: DoE Level VI effective date (2016-02-10) confirmed. CCMS/CCD database enrollment requirement confirmed as a pre-import obligation. The fragment's overall characterisation of the gap is accurate and complete.

source_url_fix: Replace source URL with: https://www.energy.ca.gov/rules-and-regulations/appliance-efficiency-regulations-title-20 (CEC Title 20 Appliance Efficiency Regulations page, confirmed live). MAEDbS login portal: https://cacertappliances.energy.ca.gov/Login.aspx. For certification assistance: https://www.energy.ca.gov/rules-and-regulations/appliance-efficiency-regulations-title-20/appliance-regulations-certification

corrections.issue: Fragment cites 'California Code of Regulations Title 20 [UNVERIFIED: specific section]'. Search results confirm external power supplies are covered under Title 20 CCR sections 1601–1608/1609. The scope is Section 1601; definitions are Section 1602; efficiency, marking, and certification requirements are in Section 1608.
corrections.action: Remove [UNVERIFIED] tag and update to: 'California Code of Regulations (CCR) Title 20, Sections 1601–1608 (Appliance Efficiency Regulations — scope, definitions, efficiency standards, marking, and certification requirements for external power supplies).'

corrections.issue: The URL cited in the fragment is a dead link (404 confirmed).
corrections.action: Replace with: https://www.energy.ca.gov/rules-and-regulations/appliance-efficiency-regulations-title-20. Publisher: 'California Energy Commission (CEC) — Appliance Efficiency Regulations Title 20'.

corrections.issue: Correctly states mandatory for California. MAEDbS registration is confirmed as a hard pre-sale gate — a regulated appliance not listed in MAEDbS cannot legally be sold or offered for sale in California.
corrections.action: No change to mandatory_status characterisation.

mandatory_status_verdict: CONFIRMED — CEC MAEDbS registration is mandatory before any external power supply (including GaN fast chargers) is sold or offered for sale in California. Non-registered products are subject to CEC enforcement.
gap_verdict: CONFIRMED — No Chinese equivalent of the CEC MAEDbS pre-sale registration database exists. Failure to register is an independent violation even if DoE Level VI thresholds are met.
notes: The dead source URL is the most important fix in this record. Title 20 CCR section numbers are now confirmed (1601-1608). All substantive claims about the MAEDbS system are accurate.

source_url_fix: Replace source URL with: https://standardscatalog.ul.com/standards/en/standard_62368-1_2 (UL Standards Catalog — UL 62368-1 4th Edition, published July 31, 2025). Publisher: 'UL Standards and Engagement — UL 62368-1 (ANSI/UL 62368-1)'.

corrections.issue: The fragment does not specify an edition in this record but ganus-safety-001 references '3rd Edition'. UL 62368-1 4th Edition (adoption of IEC 62368-1:2023) was published July 31, 2025. New certifications should reference the current edition; existing 3rd-edition certifications remain valid during transition. Verify required edition with your NRTL.
corrections.action: Update any UL 62368-1 edition reference to '3rd / 4th Edition — 4th Ed. (IEC 62368-1:2023) published July 2025; verify required edition with NRTL'.

corrections.issue: Fragment flags '[UNVERIFIED — revision number; verify at usb.org]'. Now confirmed: current USB Power Delivery Specification is Revision 3.2, Version 1.2 (released 2026-05-20 per USB-IF document library; equivalent to IEC 62680-1-2:2024). Previous long-current version was Rev 3.2 v1.1 (2024-10). Rev 3.1 is one generation behind.
corrections.action: Replace '[UNVERIFIED — revision number; verify at usb.org]' with 'Revision 3.2, Version 1.2 (USB-IF, 2026; equivalent to IEC 62680-1-2:2024) — verify latest revision at usb.org'.

corrections.issue: Fragment flags '[UNVERIFIED — revision number; verify at usb.org]'. Now confirmed: current USB Type-C Cable and Connector Specification is Release 2.4, October 2024.
corrections.action: Replace '[UNVERIFIED — revision number; verify at usb.org]' with 'Release 2.4, October 2024 (USB-IF) — verify latest revision at usb.org'.

corrections.issue: Fragment cites '29 CFR 1910.303' for the OSHA NRTL recognition programme in standards_or_laws. The foundational NRTL recognition authority is 29 CFR 1910.7; 29 CFR 1910.303(a) is the 'approved equipment' requirement that references NRTLs in the general industry electrical context.
corrections.action: Add '29 CFR 1910.7 (NRTL recognition definition and requirements — primary authority)' before 29 CFR 1910.303 in standards_or_laws.

corrections.issue: Fragment notes FCC 2025 lab restrictions in the gap section. These are now confirmed — see ganus-emc-fcc-lab-recognition-2025 verdict for full details.
corrections.action: Remove any [UNVERIFIED] hedges on the FCC 2025 China-lab restriction claim in this record.

mandatory_status_verdict: CONFIRMED — Five-layer compliance stack accurately described: NRTL (mandatory), FCC SDoC/ID (mandatory), DoE Level VI + database enrollment (mandatory federal), CEC MAEDbS (mandatory California), USB-IF (commercially mandatory via retailer contracts, not federal law).
gap_verdict: CONFIRMED — The structural gap between China's single CCC mark and the US distributed compliance stack is accurately characterised. None of the Chinese certifications transfer to any US requirement.
notes: Two dead/wrong URLs fixed (source URL and USB spec revision numbers). The substantive compliance analysis is accurate and comprehensive. The DoE multi-port rulemaking status note is appropriately hedged as of June 2026.

source_url_status: REDIRECT RISK — https://www.energy.gov/eere/buildings/external-power-supplies may redirect. The confirmed live DoE EPS page URL is https://www.energy.gov/cmei/buildings/external-power-supplies (confirmed resolves and contains the correct DoE EPS content).
source_url_fix: Replace source URL with: https://www.energy.gov/cmei/buildings/external-power-supplies. Publisher: 'U.S. Department of Energy — Office of Cybersecurity, Energy Security, and Emergency Response / Buildings (CMEI) — External Power Supplies'.

corrections.issue: The URL in the fragment uses the old /eere/ path. The correct confirmed-live URL uses the /cmei/ path.
corrections.action: Replace https://www.energy.gov/eere/buildings/external-power-supplies with https://www.energy.gov/cmei/buildings/external-power-supplies

corrections.issue: Fragment cites 'DoE Compliance Certification Database (CCD) — energy.gov'. DoE's official submission system is the Compliance Certification Management System (CCMS); the public search database is the CCD. The CCMS is the portal manufacturers/importers use for enrollment. CCD is what retailers and enforcement use to verify compliance.
corrections.action: Clarify: 'DoE Compliance Certification Management System (CCMS) and Compliance Certification Database (CCD) — energy.gov (enrolment via CCMS required before distribution in US commerce; CCD is the public verification database)'.

corrections.issue: Fragment correctly states effective date of February 10, 2016 (10 CFR Part 430 Appendix Z Level VI standards). CONFIRMED by DoE documentation.
corrections.action: No change.

corrections.issue: Fragment states 'at higher wattages (above ~49 W), the active-mode efficiency floor is approximately 89-91%'. DoE Level VI efficiency formula: Average Active Mode Efficiency >= 0.0834 * ln(Pout) + 0.526 (for output power <= 49 W) and >= 0.87 (for output power > 49 W). For higher wattages the floor is 87%, not 89-91%. The 89-91% range overstates the requirement.
corrections.action: Correct efficiency floor to: 'at higher wattages (above 49 W nameplate output), the active-mode efficiency floor is 87% (minimum average active mode efficiency = 87% for Pout > 49 W under DoE Level VI). Verify exact thresholds in 10 CFR 430.32(w) and Appendix Z.'

corrections.issue: Fragment flags '[UNVERIFIED — verify current edition at samr.gov.cn or gb.standards.com.cn]'. The edition currently in force is GB 20943-2013 (for single-phase EPS). A newer edition may exist; the [UNVERIFIED] tag is appropriate to retain.
corrections.action: Retain [UNVERIFIED] caveat on GB 20943 current edition.

mandatory_status_verdict: CONFIRMED — DoE Level VI compliance and CCMS enrollment mandatory before import or sale in US (federal, enforced via EPCA/CBP). CEC MAEDbS registration mandatory before sale in California (state law). GB 20943 does not satisfy either.
gap_verdict: CONFIRMED — Four-gap analysis (DoE test, CCMS/CCD enrollment, CEC registration, efficiency formula differences) is accurate. Efficiency floor correction from ~89-91% to 87% (for Pout > 49 W) is a material numerical fix.
notes: Two corrections: (1) source URL updated to confirmed-live /cmei/ path; (2) active-mode efficiency floor at >49 W corrected from ~89-91% to 87% per the DoE Level VI formula in 10 CFR 430.32(w). All other substantive claims confirmed.
